label_definitions.Weather Edge: Park and weather are helping home run conditions: wind, temperature, roof, air density, or HR park factor.
label_definitions.Hot Bat: Recent form is strong. It matters, but it does not override the matchup model.
label_definitions.EV Edge: The hitter's batted-ball quality is strong: exit velocity, EV50, hard-hit rate, or barrel rate.
label_definitions.Pitcher Target: The opposing pitcher is allowing HR-friendly contact, barrels, xSLG, xISO, or split damage.
label_definitions.Sleeper: A lower-profile hitter with enough data support to matter, usually with higher volatility.
label_definitions.Tough Pitcher: The hitter has real power signals, but the opposing pitcher creates resistance.
label_definitions.Confirmed: The hitter is confirmed in the starting lineup.
label_definitions.Projected: The hitter is based on projected lineup data and needs confirmation.
label_definitions.Watch: The hitter has at least one signal but not enough agreement to be a core target.
